name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in quality control, analytical chemistry, and materials characterization. Expertise in FT-IR, thermal analysis (TGA, DSC), and analytical methods for inorganic and rare earth materials. Strong background in method development, SOP creation, and maintaining quality standards in laboratory operations. Experienced in statistical process control and ensuring data integrity for QC testing.

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

bullets: Develop and validate analytical methods for materials characterization using FT-IR and thermal analysis.
Conducted materials characterization using FT-IR, TGA, DSC, and other analytical techniques.
Developed SOPs and quality control protocols for laboratory operations.
Managed QC laboratory operations including instrument calibration and maintenance.
Implemented quality control protocols ensuring data integrity.

skills.QC & Analytical: QC testing, FT-IR, TGA, DSC, method development, SOP creation
skills.Operations: Laboratory management, calibration, SPC, data integrity

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Co-led process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.
